title: Add currency selection option in Settings

requirements.text: Add a currency preference field to the backend actor that stores the user's selected currency (USD, INR, JPY) and exposes get/set query and update methods for it.

quotes: in setting add a option of different so users can select their ruppes ,dollers,yens

acceptanceCriteria: Backend stores a currency preference value per session (default: USD)
A query method getCurrency() returns the current currency preference
An update method setCurrency(currency: Text) saves the selected currency

requirements.text: Add a currency selector control in the Settings screen (SettingsScreen.tsx) that lets users choose between US Dollar (USD / $), Indian Rupee (INR / ₹), and Japanese Yen (JPY / ¥). Persist the selection to the backend and also store it locally so it survives page refreshes.

quotes: in setting add a option of different so users can select their ruppes ,dollers,yens

acceptanceCriteria: Settings screen shows a clearly labeled 'Currency' section with three selectable options: USD ($), INR (₹), JPY (¥)
The currently selected currency is visually highlighted
Selecting a currency calls the backend setCurrency method and updates local state
The selected currency persists across page refreshes

requirements.text: Update all currency formatting across the app (expense amounts in ExpenseList, TotalsCards, Dashboard, Calculator, AddExpense, and expenseMapping.ts utilities) to use the user's selected currency symbol and locale formatting (en-US for USD, en-IN for INR, ja-JP for JPY).

quotes: in setting add a option of different so users can select their ruppes ,dollers,yens

acceptanceCriteria: All displayed monetary amounts use the symbol matching the selected currency ($ / ₹ / ¥)
Number formatting uses the appropriate locale for the selected currency
Changing currency in Settings immediately reflects across all screens without requiring a page reload

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Currency selection UI must be placed within the existing SettingsScreen.tsx
nonGoals: Currency conversion of existing expense amounts (amounts are stored as-is; only the display symbol changes)
Adding currencies beyond USD, INR, and JPY
Per-expense currency selection (one global currency setting only)
